title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S16
body:
**Scene 16: 19:30–21:00 · The Knock**

HERO FRAME: Same parlor composition · Walter's hand frozen mid-air with the glass · the lamp suddenly seems too bright · the front door across the room (we see it for the first time clearly) · the door has a brass number "4-A" and a small peephole

MOTION: kling — the glass in Walter's hand trembles violently from his shock. He sets it down. Camera slowly rotates toward the door. The lamp seems to glow brighter. The door is utterly still.

MOOD: **The hinge of the entire episode.** Hold it long. Let the audience feel the impossibility.

AUDIO: Dead silence. Walter raises the glass slowly. Then —

PROSE: **KNOCK. KNOCK. KNOCK.**

Walter freezes.

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
